channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: NSCLC Survival Not Tied to Common Drugs + Immunotherapy Use
item.description: A cohort study examines whether the use of common medications is associated with worse outcomes in patients with lung cancer taking immunotherapy. Med...

item.title: Low-Dose OIT Safe for Preschoolers With Food Allergies
item.description: Low-dose oral immunotherapy is safe and generally well tolerated in preschoolers with single or multiple food allergies, new research finds. Medscape ...

item.title: Survey: 3 in 4 patients skip physical therapy homework, stunting progress
item.description: Physical therapy is a key step on the path to recovery after injuries or certain surgical procedures. A survey by The Ohio State University Wexner Med...

item.title: New Rx Option for Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder?
item.description: Phase 3 trial results suggest daratumumab, which is approved for multiple myeloma, may also be effective at reducing relapse risk in neuromyelitis opt...

item.title: National poll finds gaps in community preparedness for teen cardiac emergencies
item.description: Sudden cardiac arrest can happen to anyone, including teens, and a new national poll suggests many schools and families may not be fully prepared to r...

item.title: Indian Health Service to phase out use of dental fillings containing mercury by 2027
item.description: The federal agency that provides health care to Native Americans and Alaska Natives has announced it will phase out the use of dental fillings contain...

item.title: Bridging Therapy May Enable Early Stop to Steroids in RA
item.description: Most patients with newly diagnosed rheumatoid arthritis can successfully discontinue glucocorticoids after short-term bridging therapy, a new study sh...

item.title: As some people push to make profound autism its own diagnosis, this family is raising twins with it
item.description: Connor Murphy walked in circles around his dad, then flopped down on the kitchen floor, got up and walked in circles again.

item.title: AI model flags insulin resistance as a risk factor for 12 cancers
item.description: Insulin resistance—when the body doesn't properly respond to insulin, a hormone that helps control blood glucose levels—is one of the fundamental caus...

item.title: Fasting Improves Crohn’s Disease Symptoms and Overweight
item.description: Time-restricted feeding within an 8-hour window calms disease activity and inflammation, data indicate. Medscape News Canada

item.title: Tips for Making Exercise a Daily Habit
What Happens When Blood Sugar Falls Too Low
7 Nutrition Red Flags to Know About
item.description: Feeling unusually tired or noticing subtle changes in hair, nails, mood, or appetite? These signs may indicate nutrition gaps worth discussing with yo...

item.title: Why smaller hospitals may be faster for cancer diagnosis
item.description: In modern medicine, we like to believe that geography no longer determines outcomes. Advanced technology, integrated referral systems, and universal h...

item.title: Pediatric care in Ghana: Addressing malnutrition and sickle cell disease
item.description: In Accra, Ghana, the ward glowed ocean blue under phototherapy lamps. Chlorhexidine lingered in the air, cardboard rustled beneath a mother’s thin bla...

item.title: Missed diagnosis visceral leishmaniasis: a tragedy of note bloat

item.title: Mifepristone restrictions: How bans force patients into riskier care
item.description: I will never forget the patient I met in the emergency department who was pale, trembling, and in tears, her family in distress at her side in the tra...

item.title: Mobile wound care in 2026: Navigating regulatory pressures
item.description: I practice mobile wound care because it works. Treating chronic wounds in patients’ homes, skilled nursing facilities, and assisted living settings im...

item.title: Early menarche and later menopause are associated with increased ovarian cancer risk
item.description: 1. Across the premenopausal and postmenopausal groups, early menarche, later menopause, and longer reproductive span were associated with higher ovari...

item.title: A telephone-based intervention reduced loneliness and improved well-being among older adults
item.description: 1. Four weeks of telephone-delivered behavioral activation and mindfulness interventions by lay counsellors were associated with reduced loneliness an...

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...
